description:
You are a junior backup-engineer who has just received three historic text
  files in mixed encodings.  
  Your assignment is to create **UTF-8** working copies of those files, verify
  their integrity, and record a complete audit trail.

  1.     Pre-existing material  
         The following three source files are already present on the
         machine that the automated grader will boot for you:
           • /home/user/data/latin1_report.txt  
           • /home/user/data/shiftjis_notes.txt  
           • /home/user/data/windows1252_legacy.txt  

  2.     Your deliverables  
         a)  A UTF-8 converted copy of each source file with the exact same
             textual content but in UTF-8 encoding.  The new files must be
             placed in the same directory and must follow this naming rule:
                 <original_basename>.utf8.txt
             Examples:
               latin1_report.utf8.txt
               shiftjis_notes.utf8.txt
               windows1252_legacy.utf8.txt

         b)  One single, comma-separated log
                 /home/user/encoding_conversion/conversion_log.csv
             that summarises the work.  The file **must** contain exactly four
             columns in this order:

                 source_file,detected_encoding,target_file,sha256_utf8

             • A header row **MUST** be present (see example below).  
             • One subsequent data row per source file.  
             • Each row ends with **ONE** Unix line-feed (LF, 0x0A).  
             • Fields are plain ASCII (no embedded commas, no quotes).

             Example of layout (the concrete values will differ):

                 source_file,detected_encoding,target_file,sha256_utf8
                 latin1_report.txt,ISO-8859-1,latin1_report.utf8.txt,3d1af…
                 shiftjis_notes.txt,Shift_JIS,shiftjis_notes.utf8.txt,a1971…
                 windows1252_legacy.txt,Windows-1252,windows1252_legacy.utf8.txt,9f32b…

             The “sha256_utf8” field is the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256
             checksum of the UTF-8 file’s **bytes** (not of its textual
             contents).

  3.     Technical requirements & constraints  
         • Do not alter or delete the original three source files.  
         • The conversion must be **lossless** – every code-point that can be
           represented in UTF-8 must appear unchanged after conversion.  
         • If you need a workspace, create
               /home/user/encoding_conversion/
           (the directory does not exist yet).  
         • All commands have to run as the regular “user”; no sudo.  
         • The log must be reproducible: the grader will recompute each
           checksum and compare it to the value you recorded.  
         • After you finish, the grader will verify:
             – the presence of the three *.utf8.txt files  
             – that each of them is valid UTF-8  
             – that their textual content equals the original when the latter
               is transcoded to UTF-8 using the encoding you reported  
             – that conversion_log.csv exists, follows the exact format above,
               lists all three files, and contains correct checksums.

  4.     DO NOT reveal the contents of the existing files in this chat – discover
         everything from inside the terminal session.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth used by the automated grader.  MUST remain secret.
  -->
  <original_files>
    <file path="/home/user/data/latin1_report.txt" encoding="ISO-8859-1">
Café, crème brûlée, coöperate
Ångström unit
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/shiftjis_notes.txt" encoding="Shift_JIS">
こんにちは
バックアップ
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/windows1252_legacy.txt" encoding="Windows-1252">
“Smart quotes” and € symbol
Old file
    </file>
  </original_files>

  <utf8_targets>
    <!-- Filenames only; content will be generated by agent -->
    <file path="/home/user/data/latin1_report.utf8.txt"/>
    <file path="/home/user/data/shiftjis_notes.utf8.txt"/>
    <file path="/home/user/data/windows1252_legacy.utf8.txt"/>
  </utf8_targets>

  <log_spec>
    <path>/home/user/encoding_conversion/conversion_log.csv</path>
    <header>source_file,detected_encoding,target_file,sha256_utf8</header>
    <!--
      The grader will:
        * read every non-header row
        * verify that “detected_encoding” exactly matches the value in
          this <original_files> list
        * recompute SHA-256 of each UTF-8 file and check it against the
          sha256_utf8 column
      Therefore no literal checksum values are stored here.
    -->
  </log_spec>
name: task_000000_0e017143
